invalidation.reason: A run-owned functional-test E-Navigator Helm release still existed outside the final GitOps topology, so the frozen claim that exactly two final collector agents were present was false.
invalidation.resolution: The extra Helm release, namespace, and cluster RBAC were removed by exact name. No attempt-1 time contributes to acceptance.

Tempo, Pyroscope, Alloy, server, or client restart or replacement
configuration or image change
Prometheus or backend evidence gap that makes continuity unverifiable

invalidation.reason: The fixed-rate soak client recorded one failed request, increasing its error counter from zero to one. The zero-error acceptance condition was therefore false even though all 233099 successful requests were decoded and sent exactly and every E-Navigator and backend failure counter remained at baseline.
invalidation.diagnosis: The client harness did not retain the caught exception class. Pod identities and restart counts, Prometheus targets, resources, namespace events, node and container network-error evidence, E-Navigator accounting, and backend counters remained clean, so the recoverable classification is a single self-recovered workload transport or protocol transaction failure without E-Navigator loss.
invalidation.resolution: The run-owned harness was upgraded with fixed-cardinality exception phase/class diagnostics and per-reason counters without changing rate, request mix, timeout, image, or acceptance criteria. Both fixture deployments were rolled to new zero-restart pods before attempt 3.

fixture.reason: The controlled CPU work keeps a low-rate periodic workload observable to the production 10 Hz profiler without changing the offered rate, image, selection, or acceptance limits.

invalidation.reason: The instrumented client recorded three open_connection TimeoutError events at 2026-07-19T09:09:03Z, increasing both workload error counters from zero to three. A later transient GnuTLS symbol-resolution attachment failure independently increased the frozen optional attachment counter from zero to one.
invalidation.diagnosis: The connect phase combined asynchronous Service-name resolution and TCP establishment. The server, all targets, pod and node error/drop counters, server throttling and OOM counters, CoreDNS health, client-node TCP SYN retransmission and timeout counters, events, and warning-level node journal remained clean. The exact sub-phase is not retrospectively provable; repeated Service-name resolution is the most likely stalled operation because no TCP SYN evidence changed.
invalidation.resolution: The run-owned client now resolves the stable Service FQDN once at startup with a bounded timeout and uses the frozen ClusterIP for each TCP connection while retaining the FQDN Host header, cross-node Service routing, 15 RPS rate, 60/40 request mix, two-second request timeout, images, server work, and acceptance criteria. The phase label is now tcp_connect. Both fixture pods were rolled before attempt 4.

fixture.diagnostic_change: The client now records bounded request phase and exception class in structured logs and a fixed-cardinality error-reason counter. Offered rate, request mix, two-second timeout, images, server work, and acceptance criteria are unchanged.

invalidation.reason: The full accepted Pyroscope window was no longer queryable: 186 interior timeline samples were zero and the earliest approximately 46 minutes of attempt 4 had disappeared, so continuous useful profiling evidence from the accepted boundary could not be proven.
invalidation.diagnosis: Pyroscope had no block-storage backend and used relative default paths outside the PVC mounted at /data. Startup logged that no storage bucket was configured, runtime configuration showed an empty storage backend, and the 4 GiB PVC remained 99.378 percent free.

fixture.diagnostic_change: The client resolves the Service FQDN once before readiness and uses that frozen IPv4 ClusterIP for each TCP connection while preserving the original Host header. The runtime phase is tcp_connect. Offered rate, request mix, two-second per-operation timeout, images, server work, cross-node Service routing, and acceptance criteria are unchanged.

repair_window_counter_baselines.note: These increments occurred only while the invalidated Pyroscope backend was deliberately replaced. Their five-minute delta was zero before attempt 5 and every value is frozen as the accepted attempt-5 baseline.
